steps.assistant_text: Byte-identical TWIN-PORT of the landed kernel func_80002C08 (kernel_056). find-masked-twins.py grouped them into a strict-signature 39-insn cluster; reloc-masked word comparison showed all 39 words equal with no relocs on either side. Both are the signed 64-bit Euclidean (floored) modulo: r = a % b; if signs of r and b differ, r += b. A prior agent left it INCLUDE_ASM noting the -O2 standalone form leaf-optimizes to 33 insns vs the 39-insn target; the divergence is OPT_FLAGS, not TU-context: the kernel donor builds at -O1 -mips3 which keeps arg-homing (39 insns, 0x9c). Compiled the function in the existing -O1 -mips3 donor unit game_libs_o1_6C8AC.c (standalone .text byte-identical to target, 156 bytes, no relocs) and spliced into game_libs_post1b2c.c.o (default -O2 -mips2) via REPLACE_FUNC_BODY. The earlier '4 MIPS3 sub-function bundle / 57 words' decode comment was a splat-oversize misread.

metadata.verification: build/.o vs expected/.o function bytes byte-identical (156 bytes, 39 insns, no relocs on either side, reloc symset match); standalone -O1 -mips3 donor .text byte-identical to target; full `make` ROM byte-identical to baserom; make verify-blocks OK.
